volumeTitle: Casina. The Casket Comedy. Curculio. Epidicus. The Two Menaechmuses
label: Curculio (Section 3.1.371 - Section 3.1.458)

sections.label: Section 3.1.371
sections.translation: I seem to be in opulence ; I ’ ve struck my balance , how much money I have , and how much I owe . I ’ m rich , if I don ’ t pay [*] those to whom I ’ m in debt . If I do pay those to whom I ’ m in debt , my debts are the greatest .

sections.label: Section 3.1.375
sections.original: verum hercle vero cum belle recogito ,
sections.translation: But really , upon my faith , when I carefully consider , if they press me hard , I ’ ll resort to the Praetor [*] . Most bankers have this habit , for one to borrow of the other , and to pay nobody , and to discharge the debt with their fists , if any one duns in a loudish tone .

sections.label: Section 3.1.380
sections.translation: The person that has [*] in a short time acquired wealth , unless in good time he saves it , in good time comes to starvation . I ’ d like to buy a servant for myself , who now , however , must be sought by me on hire [*] : there ’ s occasion for my ready money .

sections.label: Section 3.1.384
sections.original: Nil tu me saturum monueris . memini et scio .
sections.translation: Don ’ t you be reminding me now I ’ m full ; I recollect and understand .

sections.label: Section 3.1.385
sections.original: ego hoc effectum lepide tibi tradam . tace .
sections.translation: I ’ ll render up to you all this cleverly carried out ; do hold your peace . I ’ faith , I ’ ve surely filled myself in-doors right well , and still in my stomach I ’ ve left room for one corner , in which to stow away the remnants of these remnants .

sections.label: Section 3.1.389
sections.original: quis hic est qui operto capite Aesculapium
sections.translation: Who ’ s this that with covered head is saluting Aesculapius ? Heyday , the very man I wanted . ( To an ATTENDANT .) Follow me .

sections.label: Section 3.1.391
sections.original: simulabo quasi non noverim . heus tu , te volo .
sections.translation: I ’ ll make pretence as though I didn ’ t know him . ( Aloud .) Hark you ; I want you .

sections.label: Section 3.1.392
sections.translation: One-eyed man [*] , save you .

sections.label: Section 3.1.392b
sections.translation: Prithee , do you jeer me ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.393
sections.original: De Coclitum prosapia te esse arbitror ,
sections.translation: I suppose that you are of the family of the Coclites [*] ; for they are one-eyed .

sections.label: Section 3.1.394b
sections.translation: This was knocked out for me by a catapulta ,

sections.label: Section 3.1.395
sections.translation: at Sicyon .

sections.label: Section 3.1.395b
sections.translation: What matters it to me , pray , if it had been knocked out by a broken pot with cinders in it ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.397
sections.original: Superstitiosus hic quidem est , véra praedicat ;
sections.translation: This fellow ’ s a wizard , surely [*] ; he tells the truth , for such catapultas are often directed at me .

sections.label: Section 3.1.399
sections.original: adulescens , ob rem publicam hoc intus mihi
sections.translation: Young man , as I bear this mark on my face [*] in the service of the public , prithee don ’ t be uncivil [*] to me .

sections.label: Section 3.1.401
sections.original: Licetne ínforare , si incomitiare non licet ?
sections.translation: May I then inforize [*] you , if I may not incomitiatize ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.402
sections.original: Non inforabis me quidem , nec mihi placet
sections.translation: You shan ’ t be inforizing me , indeed ; and really I don ’ t care at all about your Forum or your Comitia . But if you can point me out this person that I ’ m seeking ,

sections.label: Section 3.1.405
sections.original: inibis a me solidam et grandem gratiam .
sections.translation: you will be doing me a real and a great service . I ’ m looking for Lyco the banker .

sections.label: Section 3.1.406b
sections.translation: Tell me why you are now seeking for him , or , of what country are you ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.407b
sections.translation: I ’ ll tell you : I ’ m come from Therapontigonus Platagidorus , the Captain .

sections.label: Section 3.1.409
sections.original: Novi edepol nomen , nam mihi istoc nomine ,
sections.translation: I ’ faith , I know the name : ( aside ) for with that same name ,

sections.label: Section 3.1.410
sections.original: dum scribo , explevi totas ceras quattuor .
sections.translation: when I wrote , I filled four whole sides [*] . ( To CURCULIO .) But why are you seeking for Lyco ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.411b
sections.translation: I ’ ve been ordered to deliver this letter to him .

sections.label: Section 3.1.412b
sections.translation: What person are you ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.413
sections.original: Libertus illius , quem omnes Summanum vocant .
sections.translation: His freed-man , whom all call Summanus [*] .

sections.label: Section 3.1.414
sections.original: Summane , salve . qui Summanu'sSummanus es ? fac sciam .
sections.translation: Summanus , my greetings . But why Summanus ? Let me know .

sections.label: Section 3.1.415
sections.translation: Because , when in my drunken fit I ’ ve gone to sleep , I summane [*] the garments ; for that reason do all people call me Summanus .

sections.label: Section 3.1.417
sections.original: Alibi te meliust quaerere hospitium tibi :
sections.translation: ’ Twere better for you to look out for entertainment for you somewhere else ; really in my own house I have no room for a Summanus . But I am the person that you are looking for .

sections.label: Section 3.1.419b
sections.translation: Prithee , are you he ,

sections.label: Section 3.1.420
sections.translation: Lyco the banker ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.420b
sections.translation: I am .

sections.label: Section 3.1.420c
sections.translation: Therapontigonus requested me to give you a hearty greeting , and to deliver this letter .

sections.label: Section 3.1.422b
sections.translation: What , to me ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.422c
sections.translation: Just so . Take it , recognize the seal . Do you know it ? ( LYCO takes the letter .)

sections.label: Section 3.1.423b
sections.translation: Why should I not know it ? On which , a man , holding a shield , is cleaving an elephant asunder with a sword .

sections.label: Section 3.1.425
sections.original: Quod istic scriptum est , id te orare iusserat
sections.translation: What ’ s written there he bade me request you to do immediately , if you wished for his esteem .

sections.label: Section 3.1.427
sections.translation: Step aside ; I ’ ll look what ’ s written in it .

sections.label: Section 3.1.427b
sections.translation: By all means , at your pleasure , so long as I receive of you that which I ’ m come for .

sections.label: Section 3.1.429
sections.translation: Therapontigonus Platagidorus , the Captain , his guest , sends to his host Lyco , at Epidaurus ,

sections.label: Section 3.1.431
sections.translation: right hearty greeting .

sections.label: Section 3.1.431b
sections.translation: This fellow ’ s my own ; he ’ s swallowing the hook .

sections.label: Section 3.1.432
sections.original: “ Tecum oro et quaeso , qui has tabellas adferet
sections.translation: I beg and request of you that , the person who delivers this letter to you , to him be given up the girl whom I purchased there ( which I did there in your presence , and you being the negotiator ),

sections.label: Section 3.1.435
sections.original: et aurum et vestem . iam scis ut convenerit :
sections.translation: and the golden trinkets and clothes as well . You know already how it was agreed upon . You give the money to the Procurer , and give the young woman to this person . ( To CURCULIO .) Where is he himself ? Why doesn ’ t he come ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.437b
sections.translation: I ’ ll tell you ; because it is but four days since we arrived in Caria , from India ; there he now intends to order a solid golden statue

sections.label: Section 3.1.440
sections.original: solidam faciundam ex auro Philippo , quae siet
sections.translation: to be made of Philippean gold , which is to be seven feet high — a memorial of his exploits .

sections.label: Section 3.1.442
sections.translation: For what reason this ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.442b
sections.translation: I ’ ll tell you ; why , because within twenty days he singly has subdued the Persians , Paphlagonians , Sinopians , Arabians , Cretans , Syrians , Rhodia and Lycia , Peredia and Bibesia [*] ,

sections.label: Section 3.1.445
sections.translation: Centauromachia and Classia Unomammia [*] , and all Libya , and all Conterebromia ; one half even of all nations has he conquered unaided in twenty days .

sections.label: Section 3.1.449
sections.translation: Dear me !

sections.label: Section 3.1.449b
sections.translation: Why are you surprised ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.449c
sections.original: Quia enim in cavea si forent
sections.translation: Why , because if all these people were penned up in a cage

sections.label: Section 3.1.450
sections.translation: as close as chickens , even so they couldn ’ t be encompassed in a year . Upon my faith , I do believe that you are [*] come from him ; for you do jabber such nonsense .

sections.label: Section 3.1.453
sections.original: Immo etiam porro , si vis , dicam .
sections.translation: Aye , and I can tell you still more , if you like .

sections.label: Section 3.1.453b
sections.translation: No ; I don ’ t want it . Follow me this way . I ’ ll pay you that , on account of which you came ;

sections.label: Section 3.1.455
sections.translation: and lo , I see ⋯ ( from his house .) Save you , Procurer .

sections.label: Section 3.1.455b
sections.translation: May the Gods prosper you .

sections.label: Section 3.1.456
sections.original: Quid hoc quod ad te venio ?
sections.translation: Do you know what this is about which I ’ m come to you ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.456b
sections.translation: Say on what you please .

sections.label: Section 3.1.457
sections.original: Argentum accipias , cum illo mittas virginem .
sections.translation: You are to receive the money , and to send away the young woman with him . ( Pointing to CURCULIO .)

sections.label: Section 3.1.458
sections.translation: But what if I ’ m bound on oath to another ?
